---
title: Starting a herb garden
date: 2024-04-06
tags: [gardening, cooking, herbs]
---

The herbs I use most in the kitchen are the ones closest to the back door. I built a narrow bed three paces from the stove and planted it with parsley, chives, thyme, oregano, sage, and rosemary. Basil lives in a pot so I can move it out of the wind.

Most culinary herbs want full sun and poor, well-drained soil. Rich compost makes them grow fast but flabby, with weak flavor. I amend the bed once a year with coarse sand and a handful of crushed oyster shell, then leave it alone.

Harvesting often is the secret to keeping herbs productive. I cut thyme and oregano back by a third every few weeks, pinch basil tips constantly, and let a few parsley plants bolt each summer so they reseed themselves for next year.
